name: Meeting Someone New
description: Introductions, where you're from, and making connections

phrases.context: Introduce yourself — follow with your first name
A formal and friendly way to say nice to meet you — repeating it sounds warm

phrases.romanization: Hou² hoi¹ sam¹ jing⁶ sik¹ nei⁵
phrases.jyutping: hou2 hoi1 sam1 jing6 sik1 nei5
phrases.context: A warm, genuine way to express you're glad to have met someone
A very common conversation starter in multicultural HK
State your nationality — swap: 澳洲 (Australia), 美國 (USA), 加拿大 (Canada)
State your Australian nationality

phrases.romanization: Nei⁵ hai² hoeng¹ gong² zyu⁶ gei² noi⁶?
phrases.jyutping: nei5 hai2 hoeng1 gong2 zyu6 gei2 noi6
phrases.english: How long have you been in Hong Kong?
phrases.context: A standard expat conversation opener at any gathering in HK
Say how long you've lived in HK — swap in the number of years

phrases.romanization: Nei⁵ zung¹ m⁴ zung¹ ji³ hoeng¹ gong²?
phrases.jyutping: nei5 zung1 m4 zung1 ji3 hoeng1 gong2
phrases.context: Locals love asking this — have a positive answer ready
Say this with genuine warmth — HK people appreciate it
Ask about someone's job — 㗎 softens the question
State your profession — swap: 醫生 (doctor), 律師 (lawyer), 商人 (businessman)
State you're a doctor

phrases.romanization: Nei⁵ jau⁵ mou⁵ siu² pang⁴ jau⁵?
phrases.jyutping: nei5 jau5 mou5 siu2 pang4 jau5
phrases.context: A very common early question among Hong Kong parents

phrases.romanization: Ngo⁵ jau⁵ loeng⁵ go³ siu² pang⁴ jau⁵
phrases.jyutping: ngo5 jau5 loeng5 go3 siu2 pang4 jau5
phrases.context: Say how many children you have — swap the number as needed
Ask a child's age — perfectly normal to ask in HK

phrases.romanization: Keo⁵ duk⁶ bin¹ gaan¹ hok⁶ haau⁶?
phrases.jyutping: keoi5 duk6 bin1 gaan1 hok6 haau6
phrases.english: Which school does he/she go to?
phrases.context: School is a big topic for HK parents — a common early question

phrases.romanization: Nei⁵ sik¹ m⁴ sik¹ gong² gwong² dung¹ waa²?
phrases.jyutping: nei5 sik1 m4 sik1 gong2 gwong2 dung1 waa2
phrases.context: Locals are always curious and appreciative when expats learn Cantonese

words.english: can you / do you know how to

phrases.context: The humble and accurate answer for most learners — locals will always encourage you

phrases.romanization: Ngo⁵ ji⁴ gaa¹ hok⁶ gan² gwong² dung¹ waa²
phrases.jyutping: ngo5 ji4 gaa1 hok6 gan2 gwong2 dung1 waa2
phrases.context: This always gets a warm, encouraging response from locals

phrases.romanization: Nei⁵ gwong² dung¹ waa² gong² dak¹ hou² hou²
phrases.jyutping: nei5 gwong2 dung1 waa2 gong2 dak1 hou2 hou2
phrases.context: What locals will say — accept graciously!

phrases.romanization: Na⁴ leon⁴ na⁴ leon⁴, zung⁶ caa⁴ dak¹ jyun⁵
phrases.jyutping: na4 leon4 na4 leon4, zung6 caa4 dak1 jyun5
phrases.english: Not at all, I still have a long way to go
phrases.context: The humble response when someone compliments your Cantonese
Ask which area or district someone lives in
Say your neighbourhood — popular expat areas: 跑馬地 (Happy Valley), 西貢 (Sai Kung), 太古 (Taikoo)

phrases.romanization: Nei⁵ hai² ni¹ dou⁶ zou⁶ gei² noi⁶?
phrases.jyutping: nei5 hai2 ni1 dou6 zou6 gei2 noi6
phrases.english: How long will you be here?
phrases.context: Ask about someone's plans — expat postings come and go
The typical expat answer about how long you'll be in HK

words.english: we'll see / see how it goes

phrases.context: WhatsApp is universal in HK — the standard way to exchange contacts
Say you'll send a WhatsApp message

phrases.romanization: Haa⁶ ci³ jat¹ cai⁴ sik⁶ faan⁶
phrases.jyutping: haa6 ci3 jat1 cai4 sik6 faan6
phrases.context: A warm, open invitation to get together — follow through with a WhatsApp!

phrases.romanization: Ngo⁵ dei⁶ bou² ci³ lyun⁴ lok³
phrases.jyutping: ngo5 dei6 bou2 ci3 lyun4 lok3
phrases.context: Said at the end of meeting someone new — a genuine farewell sentiment

phrases.romanization: Nei⁵ hai⁶ m⁴ hai⁶ daai⁶ jat¹ ci³ lei⁴ hoeng¹ gong²?
phrases.jyutping: nei5 hai6 m4 hai6 daai6 jat1 ci3 lei4 hoeng1 gong2
phrases.chinese: 你係唔係第一次嚟香港？
phrases.english: Is this your first time in Hong Kong?
phrases.context: Ask someone who seems new to the city
Reassure someone new that they'll enjoy living here

phrases.romanization: Nei⁵ lou⁵ gung¹ / lou⁵ po⁴ hai⁶ bin¹ dou⁶ jan⁴?
phrases.jyutping: nei5 lou5 gung1 / lou5 po4 hai6 bin1 dou6 jan4
phrases.context: A common question in multicultural HK — 老公 = husband, 老婆 = wife

phrases.romanization: Ngo⁵ dei⁶ hai⁶ gwok³ zai³ gaa¹ teng¹
phrases.jyutping: ngo5 dei6 hai6 gwok3 zai3 gaa1 teng1
phrases.context: Describe your multicultural family — very common in HK
Say this to someone who's just arrived in HK

phrases.romanization: Nei⁵ jau⁵ mou⁵ hing⁴ dai⁶ ze² mui⁴?
phrases.jyutping: nei5 jau5 mou5 hing4 dai6 ze2 mui4
phrases.context: Ask about someone's family
Say you're an only child — 獨生子 (duk6 sang1 zi2) for male

phrases.romanization: Nei⁵ gaa¹ jan⁴ hai² hoeng¹ gong² maa³?
phrases.jyutping: nei5 gaa1 jan4 hai2 hoeng1 gong2 maa3
phrases.english: Is your family here in Hong Kong?
phrases.context: Ask if someone's family is with them or back home

phrases.romanization: Nei⁵ wui⁵ m⁴ wui⁵ faan¹ hoeng¹ haa¹?
phrases.jyutping: nei5 wui5 m4 wui5 faan1 hoeng1 haa1
phrases.english: Do you go back to your home country often?
phrases.context: Ask if someone visits their home country regularly
The typical expat answer about trips home

dialogueScenes.title: Meeting someone at a party
dialogueScenes.description: You're at a gathering and meet someone for the first time

turns.speaker: other
turns.jyutping: nei5 hou2, ngo5 giu3 May.
turns.romanization: Nei⁵ hou², ngo⁵ giu³ May.
turns.english: Hi, I'm May.

turns.speaker: user
turns.chinese: 你好！我叫Sarah。好開心認識你。
turns.jyutping: nei5 hou2! ngo5 giu3 Sarah. hou2 hoi1 sam1 jing6 sik1 nei5.
turns.romanization: Nei⁵ hou²! Ngo⁵ giu³ Sarah. Hou² hoi¹ sam¹ jing⁶ sik¹ nei⁵.
turns.english: Hi! I'm Sarah. Really nice to meet you.

turns.speaker: other
turns.jyutping: nei5 hai6 bin1 dou6 jan4 gaa3?
turns.romanization: Nei⁵ hai⁶ bin¹ dou⁶ jan⁴ gaa³?
turns.english: Where are you from?

turns.speaker: user
turns.jyutping: ngo5 hai6 jing1 gwok3 jan4. nei5 ne1?
turns.romanization: Ngo⁵ hai⁶ jing¹ gwok³ jan⁴. Nei⁵ ne¹?
turns.english: I'm British. And you?

turns.speaker: other
turns.chinese: 我係香港人。你喺香港住幾耐？
turns.jyutping: ngo5 hai6 hoeng1 gong2 jan4. nei5 hai2 hoeng1 gong2 zyu6 gei2 noi6?
turns.romanization: Ngo⁵ hai⁶ hoeng¹ gong² jan⁴. Nei⁵ hai² hoeng¹ gong² zyu⁶ gei² noi⁶?
turns.english: I'm from Hong Kong. How long have you been here?

turns.speaker: user
turns.chinese: 我住咗兩年。好鍾意香港！
turns.jyutping: ngo5 zyu6 zo2 loeng5 nin4. hou2 zung1 ji3 hoeng1 gong2!
turns.romanization: Ngo⁵ zyu⁶ zo² loeng⁵ nin⁴. Hou² zung¹ ji³ hoeng¹ gong²!
turns.english: Two years. I love Hong Kong!

dialogueScenes.title: Your Cantonese gets complimented
dialogueScenes.description: You use a Cantonese phrase and someone praises you

turns.speaker: user
turns.jyutping: ngo5 sik1 siu2 siu2 gwong2 dung1 waa2.
turns.romanization: Ngo⁵ sik¹ siu² siu² gwong² dung¹ waa².
turns.english: I know a little Cantonese.

turns.speaker: other
turns.chinese: 哇，你廣東話講得好好喎！
turns.jyutping: waa3, nei5 gwong2 dung1 waa2 gong2 dak1 hou2 hou2 wo3!
turns.romanization: Waa³, nei⁵ gwong² dung¹ waa² gong² dak¹ hou² hou² wo³!
turns.english: Wow, your Cantonese is really good!

turns.speaker: user
turns.chinese: 哪裡哪裡，仲差得遠。我而家學緊。
turns.jyutping: na4 leon4 na4 leon4, zung6 caa4 dak1 jyun5. ngo5 ji4 gaa1 hok6 gan2.
turns.romanization: Na⁴ leon⁴ na⁴ leon⁴, zung⁶ caa⁴ dak¹ jyun⁵. Ngo⁵ ji⁴ gaa¹ hok⁶ gan².
turns.english: Not at all, I still have a long way to go. I'm learning now.

turns.speaker: other
turns.jyutping: hou2 lei6 hoi6! gaa1 jau4!
turns.romanization: Hou² lei⁶ hoi⁶! Gaa¹ jau⁴!
turns.english: That's impressive! Keep it up!

name: School Gate Chat
description: Morning drop-offs, playdates, and parent conversations

phrases.romanization: Nei⁵ go³ zai² hai⁶ gei² nin⁴ kap¹?
phrases.jyutping: nei5 go3 zai2 hai6 gei2 nin4 kap1
phrases.english: What year is your son in?
phrases.context: Ask which year or class a child is in — 個仔 = son, 個女 = daughter
HK primary school goes P1–P6, secondary is S1–S6

phrases.english: What time do they finish today?
phrases.context: Ask about pick-up time — it can vary on different days

words.english: finish at what time / let out

phrases.romanization: Nei⁵ gam¹ jat⁶ heoi³ m⁴ heoi³ zip³?
phrases.jyutping: nei5 gam1 jat6 heoi3 m4 heoi3 zip3
phrases.context: Ask if another parent is collecting their child

phrases.romanization: Ngo⁵ gam¹ jat⁶ m⁴ dak¹, fei¹ jung⁶ lei⁴ zip³
phrases.jyutping: ngo5 gam1 jat6 m4 dak1, fei1 jung6 lei4 zip3
phrases.english: I can't make it today, the helper is picking up
phrases.context: Explain that your domestic helper is doing school pick-up

phrases.romanization: Nei⁵ go³ zai² giu³ me¹ meng²?
phrases.jyutping: nei5 go3 zai2 giu3 me1 meng2
phrases.context: Ask a child's name — swap 個仔 (son) for 個女 (daughter)
Ask a child's age

phrases.romanization: Ngo⁵ dei⁶ ho² m⁴ ho² ji³ on¹ paai⁴ playtime?
phrases.jyutping: ngo5 dei6 ho2 m4 ho2 ji3 on1 paai4 playtime
phrases.context: The school gate classic — 'playtime' is the universal HK English term

phrases.romanization: Nei⁵ haa⁶ go³ sing¹ kei⁴ luk⁶ dak¹ m⁴ dak¹?
phrases.jyutping: nei5 haa6 go3 sing1 kei4 luk6 dak1 m4 dak1
phrases.english: Is next Saturday okay for you?
phrases.context: Suggest a specific day for the playdate
Agree to the playdate and ask what time
Suggest a time for the playdate
Follow up by sending details on WhatsApp

phrases.romanization: Gam¹ jat⁶ hai⁶ m⁴ hai⁶ jau⁵ gung¹ fo³?
phrases.jyutping: gam1 jat6 hai6 m4 hai6 jau5 gung1 fo3
phrases.context: Commiserate with fellow parents over homework
Commiserate with other parents — HK schools are known for heavy homework

phrases.romanization: Hok⁶ haau⁶ gam¹ go³ jyut⁶ jau⁵ me¹ wui⁶?
phrases.jyutping: hok6 haau6 gam1 go3 jyut6 jau5 me1 wui6
phrases.english: What events does the school have this month?
phrases.context: Stay up to date with school events

phrases.romanization: Wan⁶ dung⁶ wui² hai⁶ gei² si⁴?
phrases.jyutping: wan6 dung6 wui2 hai6 gei2 si4
phrases.context: Ask about upcoming school events

phrases.romanization: Nei⁵ jau⁵ mou⁵ jap⁶ dou³ gaa¹ zoeng² kwan⁴?
phrases.jyutping: nei5 jau5 mou5 jap6 dou3 gaa1 zoeng2 kwan4
phrases.english: Are you in the parent group chat?
phrases.context: Ask if someone has joined the class WhatsApp group
Add someone to the class parent group on WhatsApp
Compliment someone's child — 靚 for girls, 靚仔 for boys
The humble Cantonese response when someone compliments your child

phrases.romanization: Keo⁵ dei⁶ hai⁶ hou² pang⁴ jau⁵
phrases.jyutping: keoi5 dei6 hai6 hou2 pang4 jau5
phrases.context: Comment on how well your children get along

phrases.romanization: Nei⁵ dei⁶ jau⁵ mou⁵ heoi³ faan⁴ soeng³ wui²?
phrases.jyutping: nei5 dei6 jau5 mou5 heoi3 faan4 soeng3 wui2
phrases.english: Are you going to the farewell party?
phrases.context: Ask about end-of-year or leaving events — common in expat school communities
Tell another school parent you're leaving the area or HK
Respond with surprise when someone says they're leaving

phrases.english: I'll really miss it / That's so sad to hear
phrases.context: Express that you'll miss someone or something — a genuine Cantonese sentiment

words.english: can't bear to leave / will miss

phrases.romanization: Gei³ dak¹ bou² ci³ lyun⁴ lok³
phrases.jyutping: gei3 dak1 bou2 ci3 lyun4 lok3
phrases.context: A heartfelt farewell — common in the transient expat community

phrases.romanization: Toi¹ ngo⁵ man⁶ hau⁶ nei⁵ lou⁵ gung¹
phrases.jyutping: toi1 ngo5 man6 hau6 nei5 lou5 gung1
phrases.english: Say hi to your husband for me
phrases.context: A warm closing — swap 老公 (husband) for 老婆 (wife) as appropriate

phrases.romanization: Hou¹ dei¹ hoeng¹! Haa⁶ ci³ gin³!
phrases.jyutping: hou1 dei1 hoeng1! haa6 ci3 gin3!
phrases.context: A cheerful farewell at the school gate

dialogueScenes.title: Morning drop-off chat
dialogueScenes.description: You meet another parent at the school gate

turns.speaker: other
turns.jyutping: zou2 san4! nei5 go3 zai2 hai6 gei2 nin4 kap1?
turns.romanization: Zou² san⁴! Nei⁵ go³ zai² hai⁶ gei² nin⁴ kap¹?
turns.english: Good morning! What year is your son in?

turns.speaker: user
turns.jyutping: zou2 san4! keoi5 hai6 P ji6. nei5 go3 neoi5 ne1?
turns.romanization: Zou² san⁴! Keo⁵ hai⁶ P ji⁶. Nei⁵ go³ neoi⁵ ne¹?
turns.english: Good morning! He's in P2. And your daughter?

turns.speaker: other
turns.jyutping: keoi5 dou1 hai6 P ji6! hai6 m4 hai6 tung4 baan1?
turns.romanization: Keo⁵ dou¹ hai⁶ P ji⁶! Hai⁶ m⁴ hai⁶ tung⁴ baan¹?
turns.english: She's in P2 too! Are they in the same class?

turns.speaker: user
turns.chinese: 係呀！我哋可唔可以安排playtime？
turns.jyutping: hai6 aa3! ngo5 dei6 ho2 m4 ho2 ji3 on1 paai4 playtime?
turns.romanization: Hai⁶ aa³! Ngo⁵ dei⁶ ho² m⁴ ho² ji³ on¹ paai⁴ playtime?
turns.english: Yes! Can we arrange a playdate?

turns.speaker: other
turns.jyutping: hou2 aa3! ngo5 send nei5 WhatsApp.
turns.romanization: Hou² aa³! Ngo⁵ send nei⁵ WhatsApp.
turns.english: Sure! I'll message you on WhatsApp.

turns.speaker: user
turns.jyutping: hou2! haa6 ci3 gin3!
turns.romanization: Hou²! Haa⁶ ci³ gin³!
turns.english: Great! See you next time!

dialogueScenes.title: Arranging a playdate
dialogueScenes.description: Following up to plan the kids' get-together

turns.speaker: user
turns.jyutping: nei5 haa6 go3 sing1 kei4 luk6 dak1 m4 dak1?
turns.romanization: Nei⁵ haa⁶ go³ sing¹ kei⁴ luk⁶ dak¹ m⁴ dak¹?
turns.english: Is next Saturday okay for you?

turns.speaker: other
turns.jyutping: dak1 aa3! gei2 dim2 hou2?
turns.romanization: Dak¹ aa³! Gei² dim² hou²?
turns.english: Yes! What time is good?

turns.speaker: user
turns.jyutping: haa6 zau3 loeng5 dim2? lei4 ngo5 dei6 uk1 kei2.
turns.romanization: Haa⁶ zau³ loeng⁵ dim². Lei⁴ ngo⁵ dei⁶ uk¹ kei².
turns.english: 2pm? Come to our place.

turns.speaker: other
turns.chinese: 好呀，多謝。你哋住喺邊度？
turns.jyutping: hou2 aa3, do1 ze6. nei5 dei6 zyu6 hai2 bin1 dou6?
turns.romanization: Hou² aa³, do¹ ze⁶. Nei⁵ dei⁶ zyu⁶ hai² bin¹ dou⁶?
turns.english: Great, thanks. Where do you live?

turns.speaker: user
turns.jyutping: ngo5 zyu6 hai2 bun3 saan1. ngo5 send nei5 dei6 zi2.
turns.romanization: Ngo⁵ zyu⁶ hai² bun³ saan¹. Ngo⁵ send nei⁵ dei⁶ zi².
turns.english: Mid-Levels. I'll send you the address.

name: Celebrations
description: Birthdays, Lunar New Year, weddings, and toasts

phrases.context: The universal birthday greeting — works for all ages

phrases.romanization: Zuk¹ nei⁵ san¹ tai² gin⁶ hong¹
phrases.jyutping: zuk1 nei5 san1 tai2 gin6 hong1
phrases.context: A warm birthday or New Year wish — health is deeply valued in HK culture
THE Lunar New Year greeting — expect it from everyone from January onwards
For both the Gregorian and Lunar New Year
Children say this to adults at CNY — it's expected and always gets a laugh

words.english: hand it over / give it here

phrases.context: A versatile congratulations for any good news
Wedding congratulations

phrases.english: A hundred years of happiness together
phrases.context: The classic wedding wish in Cantonese culture
Said around the Mid-Autumn Festival — celebrated with mooncakes and lanterns

phrases.romanization: Ni¹ go³ lai⁵ mat⁶ sung³ bei² nei⁵
phrases.jyutping: ni1 go3 lai5 mat6 sung3 bei2 nei5
phrases.context: Present a gift to someone

phrases.romanization: Nei⁵ m⁴ sai² gam³ haak³ hei³
phrases.jyutping: nei5 m4 sai2 gam3 haak3 hei3
phrases.context: Gracious response when receiving a gift — always say this before opening it
Express deep gratitude — more heartfelt than just 多謝

words.english: thank you so much (完全感謝)

phrases.context: Toast with everyone at the table — glasses clink, everyone drinks
Literally 'dry the cup' — the formal toast at banquets and celebrations

phrases.romanization: Zuk¹ nei⁵ gung¹ zok³ seon⁶ lei⁶
phrases.jyutping: zuk1 nei5 gung1 zok3 seon6 lei6
phrases.context: A common New Year or farewell wish
A warm, all-encompassing wish for someone's wellbeing
Said to children — one of the most common blessings for kids in HK

phrases.english: May all your wishes come true
phrases.context: A powerful New Year blessing — literally 'ten thousand things go as you wish'

phrases.english: May your heart's desires come true
phrases.context: A beautiful and common New Year wish
Wish someone well when they're moving to a new home
A versatile blessing — used for travel, life events, and general wellbeing
A warm compliment at a social gathering

dialogueScenes.title: Lunar New Year visit
dialogueScenes.description: You visit a colleague's family during CNY

turns.speaker: user
turns.jyutping: gung1 hei2 faat3 coi4! san1 nin4 faai3 lok3!
turns.romanization: Gung¹ hei² faat³ coi⁴! San¹ nin⁴ faai³ lok³!
turns.english: Wishing you prosperity! Happy New Year!

turns.speaker: other
turns.jyutping: gung1 hei2 faat3 coi4! jap6 lai4 co5, sik6 je5.
turns.romanization: Gung¹ hei² faat³ coi⁴! Jap⁶ lai⁴ co⁵, sik⁶ je⁵.
turns.english: Wishing you prosperity! Come in, have something to eat.

turns.speaker: user
turns.chinese: 呢個小小禮物送俾你哋。
turns.jyutping: ni1 go3 siu2 siu2 lai5 mat6 sung3 bei2 nei5 dei6.
turns.romanization: Ni¹ go³ siu² siu² lai⁵ mat⁶ sung³ bei² nei⁵ dei⁶.
turns.english: This small gift is for you all.

turns.speaker: other
turns.jyutping: nei5 m4 sai2 gam3 haak3 hei3! do1 ze6 saai3!
turns.romanization: Nei⁵ m⁴ sai² gam³ haak³ hei³! Do¹ ze⁶ saai³!
turns.english: You really didn't have to! Thank you so much!

turns.speaker: user
turns.chinese: 祝你哋萬事如意，心想事成！
turns.jyutping: zuk1 nei5 dei6 maan6 si6 jyu4 ji3, sam1 soeng2 si6 sing4!
turns.romanization: Zuk¹ nei⁵ dei⁶ maan⁶ si⁶ jyu⁴ ji³, sam¹ soeng² si⁶ sing⁴!
turns.english: Wishing you all a wonderful year ahead!

dialogueScenes.title: Birthday party toast
dialogueScenes.description: You're at a friend's birthday dinner

turns.speaker: other
turns.jyutping: do1 ze6 daai6 gaa1 lei4!
turns.romanization: Do¹ ze⁶ daai⁶ gaa¹ lei⁴!
turns.english: Thank you all for coming!

turns.speaker: user
turns.chinese: 生日快樂！祝你身體健康，萬事如意！
turns.jyutping: saang1 jat6 faai3 lok3! zuk1 nei5 san1 tai2 gin6 hong1, maan6 si6 jyu4 ji3!
turns.romanization: Saang¹ jat⁶ faai³ lok³! Zuk¹ nei⁵ san¹ tai² gin⁶ hong¹, maan⁶ si⁶ jyu⁴ ji³!
turns.english: Happy birthday! Wishing you good health and all the best!

turns.speaker: other
turns.jyutping: do1 ze6! ngo5 dei6 gon1 bui1!
turns.romanization: Do¹ ze⁶! Ngo⁵ dei⁶ gon¹ bui¹!
turns.english: Thank you! Let's toast!

turns.speaker: user
turns.jyutping: gon1 bui1!
turns.romanization: Gon¹ bui¹!
turns.english: Cheers!

name: Small Talk
description: Weather, weekend plans, compliments, and everyday chit-chat

phrases.romanization: Gam¹ jat⁶ tin¹ hei³ zan¹ hai⁶ hou² hou²
phrases.jyutping: gam1 jat6 tin1 hei3 zan1 hai6 hou2 hou2
phrases.english: The weather is so nice today
phrases.context: A universal conversation opener — especially meaningful after a typhoon
The most relatable thing you can say in HK from May to October
HK has sudden downpours — knowing this phrase is practical
Typhoon season news spreads fast — this is a very common phrase
Signal 8 means work and school close — a very important question in HK

phrases.romanization: Nei⁵ zau¹ mut⁶ jau⁵ me¹ ze⁶ muk⁶?
phrases.jyutping: nei5 zau1 mut6 jau5 me1 ze6 muk6
phrases.english: What are your plans this weekend?
phrases.context: A natural conversation starter — HK weekends are busy
HK has amazing trails — hiking is a beloved local weekend activity

phrases.romanization: Nei⁵ gam¹ jat⁶ leng³ zo² hou² do¹
phrases.jyutping: nei5 gam1 jat6 leng3 zo2 hou2 do1
phrases.english: You look so much nicer today
phrases.context: Compliment someone's appearance — Cantonese people are direct with compliments
Not considered rude in HK — it's a compliment! People will ask you directly

phrases.romanization: Nei⁵ dei⁶ gang¹ gang¹ haak³ faan¹ lei⁴?

phrases.english: Did you just get back from travelling?

Ask where someone travelled to
Check in with someone you haven't seen for a while

words.english: how is it / how have you been

phrases.english: Same as always / Not much has changed
phrases.context: A very typical Cantonese response — genuine contentment with the status quo
When you run into someone you haven't seen in a while

phrases.romanization: Nei⁵ zan¹ hai⁶ leng³ zo² ge³

phrases.english: You're genuinely glowing / You really look great
phrases.context: A sincere, stronger compliment when someone looks noticeably well

phrases.context: A warm close to any chat — in HK, meals are how relationships are maintained
